summary: Running skill_advisor.py across all 200 prompts produced 107 exact matches (53.5% accuracy), with false_fire=10, missed_skill=32, and wrong_skill=51.
Accuracy is highest on synthetic-realistic prompts (68.0%) and collapses to 0.0% on synthetic-command prompts because all 12 route to the wrong surface.
Command-only mirrors account for 14 false positives even though the gold corpus only labels packet skills, not command surfaces, as the top-1 answer.
The gold system-spec-kit surface only recalls 15 of 55 prompts because packet-local maintenance prompts fragment across sk-code-review, command surfaces, mcp-chrome-devtools, and none.

Command-only mirrors over-fire even though the gold corpus expects packet skills, producing 14 false positives across command-memory-save, command-spec-kit-resume, command-spec-kit-deep-research, and command-spec-kit-deep-review.
sk-code-opencode only recalls 3 of 17 gold prompts; half fall to none and the rest drift into unrelated skills such as sk-code-web, mcp-coco-index, and sk-improve-prompt.
sk-improve-prompt over-fires on review, research, and packet-maintenance prompts, producing 12 false positives and only 42.9% precision.
mcp-chrome-devtools fires on packet-local routing prompts that are either read-only or should stay in system-spec-kit, yielding 9 false positives and only 25.0% precision.
